name: Winter Squash Tart
recipeIngredient: 2 medium leeks (about 4-5 oz), mostly white parts
1/3 cup creme fraiche or heavy cream
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. Cut the squash in half, remove the seeds and pulp. Brush the surface with oil and season with salt and pepper. Place the halves, cut side down, on a baking sheet, and bake until the skin is puffed and the squash is soft, about an hour.
3. Remove from oven, let cool enough to handle easily, then scoop out the flesh. Pass through a food mill or chinois to smooth texture; reserve about 1-1/2 cups. Freeze any extra.
4. While squash bakes, prepare tart dough. Partially bake and set aside.
5. Trim leeks, quarter lengthwise, slice into 1/4-inch squares (slice works too) and wash well. Melt butter in a skillet, add leeks, and cook over medium-high heat until tender, 7 to 10 minutes. Add a little water after the first 3 or 4 minutes to prevent burning.
6. Season with salt.
7. Beat eggs in a bowl with creme fraiche or cream, milk, and squash. Stir in leeks, grated cheese, herbs. Season to taste with salt, pepper, and nutmeg.
8. Set oven at 375°F. Pour batter into tart shell and bake in the center until firm and browned from melted cheese, about 45 to 50 minutes.
9. Remove from oven, let rest 5 to 10 minutes before serving on a platter.
Makes one 9- or 10-inch tart (10-inch quiche dish).
Notes: Squash can be prepared ahead. Actual kitchen work is minimal once squash is prepped; nice for company.
